---
title: "About, attribution, and errata"
toc: true
---

## The original paper

> M. Geshkovski, P. Rigollet, Y. Ruiz-Balet. *Measure-to-measure interpolation using Transformers.*
> arXiv:2411.04551v3.

All theorems, propositions, lemmas, and their proofs are the work of the original authors. Please
cite their paper for the mathematics.

## This project

The Lean 4 formalization, the labeled axiom layer, the numerical experiments, the prose
informalizations, this website, and the blueprint are the work of **Alexandre Quemy**. The repository
is [`aquemy/measure-to-measure-transformers`](https://github.com/aquemy/measure-to-measure-transformers).

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple"}
**Attribution and fair use.** Throughout the blueprint, the original authors' statements and proofs
are reproduced **verbatim** in clearly marked, page-cited grey boxes (`From the original paper`),
solely for scholarly comparison with the formalization. That material remains the authors'. Our
contributions (Lean, prose informalizations, experiments) appear in the blue and green boxes.
:::

## What the formalization strengthens

### F1 -- a sign error in the gate of Lemma B.2 {#f1}

The proof of Lemma B.2 (Appendix B, p. 31, eq. (B.4)) takes parameters
$U = -z\mathbf 1^\top$, $b = \cos(R)\mathbf 1$, giving the gate $(\cos R - \langle z,x\rangle)_+$, and
asserts it is positive exactly on the ball $\mathcal B_0 = B(z,R)$. But
$$\cos R - \langle z,x\rangle > 0 \iff \langle z,x\rangle < \cos R \iff d_g(z,x) > R \iff x \notin \mathcal B_0,$$
so the printed gate is active on the **complement** of $\mathcal B_0$; as written, the interior mass
sees a zero gate and the lemma transports nothing. The fix is a one-sign typo:
$U = +z\mathbf 1^\top$, $b = -\cos(R)\mathbf 1$. The error is **typographical and recoverable** -- no
theorem (Prop 2.2, Theorems 1.1, 1.2) is affected once the sign is flipped.

It is corroborated three ways: (i) the paper's own Proposition 4.2, Step 3 (p. 22) uses the same gate
and states it with the correct (opposite) side; (ii) the kernel-checked leaf
`MeasureToMeasure.Leaves.gate_pos_iff_dist` (L2) proves the gate is active on $\{d_g(z,x)>R\}$; and
(iii) experiment E1 failed (mass fraction $\approx 0.27$) when seeding mass inside $B(z,R)$ as the
printed (B.4) suggests, and passed (fraction $1.0$) only on the gate-active region L2 identifies.

The full erratum note (written so it can be sent to the authors as a courtesy) is in
[`ERRATA.md`](https://github.com/aquemy/measure-to-measure-transformers/blob/main/ERRATA.md).

### F2 -- a rigor gap in Proposition 3.1's induction {#f2}

The induction proving Proposition 3.1 asserts the step "disjoint geodesic hulls $\Rightarrow$
non-colinear barycenters" without proof. The formalization supplies it as the machine-checked leaf
`barycenter_noncolinear_of_disjoint_hull` (L11) for the empirical regime, and
`barycenter_noncolinear_of_disjoint_hull_general` (L11$'$, via Mathlib's `Convex.integral_mem`) for
arbitrary probability measures, with no new axioms.

## Honesty: what is proved, axiomatised, or open

Every node carries a status badge driven by the Lean kernel (`#print axioms`, via `bin/axiom-report`):

- [machine-checked]{.badge .ok} -- clean kernel proof (`propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound` only);
- [axiomatised]{.badge .axiom} -- rests on a labeled axiom standing in for mean-field / dynamical theory missing from Mathlib;
- [open]{.badge .open} -- stated in Lean as a `sorry`;
- [informal]{.badge .informal} -- reviewed on paper, not yet stated in Lean.

A node is never badged above its true kernel status. The two headline theorems are **proved by
assembling** the mid-level results over the axiom layer (zero `sorry`), so their effective status is
[axiomatised]{.badge .axiom} -- clean on their own logic, but resting on the labeled mean-field /
dynamical axioms. Kernel honesty is necessary but not sufficient: the kernel checks each statement
*as written*, never that it faithfully transcribes the paper. So every axiom additionally passes a
statement-fidelity admission protocol (verbatim source anchor, hypothesis-parity diff, adversarial
refutation attempt, model-adequacy check; `Source-Ref:` / `Refutation-Attempt:` commit footers per
CKC spec v0.2.0, enforced by the pre-commit hook) and is guarded by a committed **refutation
regression suite**: the disproofs of previously-refuted draft statements are kernel-checked
theorems, paired with must-fail adapters that CI compiles on every push, and every axiom carries a
compiled non-vacuity witness (concrete data satisfying all its hypotheses), so an over-strengthened
statement breaks the build just as a re-loosened one trips the gate. The self-contained leaves
and the whole Appendix-B transport chain carry the machine-checked load; see
**[Beyond Mathlib](beyond-mathlib.qmd)** for the plan to discharge the remaining axioms and lift
the headline results to [machine-checked]{.badge .ok}.

## Licensing

The original paper is the authors'. The formalization and experiment code in this repository are
released under the repository's license; verbatim paper excerpts are included under fair use for
scholarly comparison and remain the property of the original authors.
